channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lebo M takes legal action over misrepresentation of The Lion King chant
item.description: Grammy-winning South African composer Lebohang Morake, popularly known as Lebo M, has filed a lawsuit in the United States seeking between $20 million and $27 million in damages against Zimbabwean com...

item.title: Ottawa temporarily halts deportation of Montreal father and son amid family separation concerns
item.description: A Montreal father, Ravi Chauhan, and his son were granted a temporary reprieve from deportation after the Public Safety Minister intervened just days before their scheduled removal from Canada. Their ...

item.title: Nigel Farage’s Cameo videos reveal connections to far-right online culture
item.description: Over the past five years, Nigel Farage has utilised digital platforms like TikTok and Cameo to expand his political influence and reach new audiences. While Cameos are personalised videos intended for...

item.title: Global oil prices increase after US-Iran tensions escalate
item.description: Global oil prices have experienced a noticeable increase following a recent ultimatum issued by former US President Donald Trump concerning Iran. Analysts note that such political developments can cre...

item.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator – Channel 4 reality show review
item.description: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator is Channel 4’s new nine-week reality competition series that blends elements of The Traitors with the arena-based survival of The Hunger Games. Ten contestants are placed in...

item.title: Assam Citizens' Forum Calls for Opposition Unity Against BJP in Assembly Elections
item.description: The Assam Nagorik Sanmilani, a citizens' forum led by academician Hiren Gohain and retired IPS officer Harekrishna Deka, has urged voters to unite against the BJP in the upcoming Assam Assembly electi...

item.title: One Nation's rise in South Australia challenges the Liberal Party and signals risks for Labor
item.description: In South Australia's March 2026 election, One Nation performed better than expected, surpassing the Liberal Party in popular vote. While the Liberals may edge ahead in seat count, the rise of Pauline ...

item.title: Cabinet Committee on Security reviews India's preparedness amid West Asia conflict’s economic effects
item.description: The Cabinet Committee on Security (CCS), chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, held a meeting on Sunday to assess how the ongoing conflict in West Asia could affect India’s economy. Recognising the...

item.title: Carabao Cup Final Referee Peter Bankes Barred from Officiating Liverpool and Everton Due to County FA Affiliation
item.description: The Carabao Cup final took place on Sunday, March 22, 2026, at Wembley Stadium, where Manchester City defeated Arsenal 2-0 to lift the trophy. Nico O'Reilly was the standout performer for City, scorin...

item.title: Punjab Opposition Pressures Government for Arrest of Former AAP Minister after Official’s Death
item.description: Opposition parties in Punjab — including the Congress, Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), and Shiromani Akali Dal (SAD) — have intensified their demand for the arrest of senior Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) leader...

item.title: Asake's Journey to OAU Ife: His Mother's Account of His Decision to Study in Nigeria
item.description: Asake's mother recently shared an emotional story on Korty EO’s YouTube podcast about how her son chose to study at Obafemi Awolowo University (OAU) in Ile-Ife, Osun State, over going to the United Ki...

item.title: De La Salle University Ties for Top Spot in UAAP Baseball Standings with Win Over Ateneo
item.description: In the UAAP Season 88 baseball tournament, De La Salle University secured a crucial victory against archrival Ateneo de Manila University, enabling them to climb to a share of the lead in the team sta...

item.title: 14 hikers rescued from Mount Brunswick after falls, 1 in critical condition
item.description: A rescue operation on Mount Brunswick in British Columbia successfully saved 14 hikers, including two who had fallen in snowy conditions. The North Shore Rescue team responded to the call from Lions B...

item.title: Gov Radda Assures EU Diplomats That Katsina State Provides Enabling Environment for Investments and Business Growth
item.description: Gov. Dikko Radda of Katsina State don assure international partners, especially European Union ambassadors and diplomats, say Katsina remain one of the most business-friendly states for Nigeria. The g...

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns advance to CAF Champions League semifinals despite red card
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns secured their place in the CAF Champions League semifinals after a tense second-leg encounter with Stade Malien in Bamako, Mali. Coming off a comfortable 3-0 home victory from the fi...

item.title: Mel Barrera-Montenegro’s Journey Highlights Resilience in ‘What Lies Beneath’
item.description: The latest ABS-CBN television drama series, “What Lies Beneath,” tells a compelling story of resilience and the enduring strength of women through the character of Mel Barrera-Montenegro. Played by Su...

item.title: US Counterterrorism Director Resigns Over Iran War, Citing Israeli Influence
item.description: In a significant development amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict, Joe Kent, director of the National Counterterrorism Center, resigned on March 18, 2026, becoming the first senior Trump administration o...

item.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank's Subsidiary Sells Nearly 31% Stake in Infina Finance for Rs 1,294 Crore
item.description: Kotak Mahindra Bank announced that its wholly-owned subsidiary, Kotak Mahindra Capital Company (KMCC), has entered into agreements to sell approximately 30.99% stake in Infina Finance Private Limited,...

item.title: Zanzibar Launches $300 Million Mangapwani Integrated Port to Tackle Port Congestion, Power and Fuel Storage Challenges

item.title: Tinubu Vows to Tackle Insecurity as Key Priority
